articles.title: All Points signs agreement to build payload processing facilities at KSC
articles.summary:
All Points Logistics has signed an agreement with NASA’s Kennedy Space Center to construct satellite processing facilities on center property.

articles.title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
articles.summary: The Republic of Latvia will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at 9 a.m. EDT Monday, April 20, at NASA Headquarters in Washington. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host Dace Melbārde, Latvia’s minister for education and science; Jānis Beķeris, chargé d’affaires at the Embassy of the Republic of Latvia to the United States; and […]

articles.title: How space weather forecasting keeps astronauts (and satellites) safe
articles.summary:
BOULDER, Colorado – The sun’s volatile outbursts, such as storms, flares and other space weather, can cause serious harm to astronauts like the Artemis 2 crew who recently came home, and to satellites. That’s why the Space Weather Prediction Center (SWPC) in Boulder, Colorado — part of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration — is […]
The post How space weather forecasting keeps astronauts (and satellites) safe appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA seeks commercial and exploration links for new Earth science missions
articles.summary:
Two new proposed NASA Earth science missions will attempt to address key research topics while leveraging both commercial and exploration capabilities. During NASA’s “Ignition” event March 24, the agency announced two new Earth science mission concepts, called EAGLE and FALCON. The announcements were largely overlooked at an event that focused on development of a lunar […]
The post NASA seeks commercial and exploration links for new Earth science missions appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: At the Edge of Light
articles.summary: In this photo taken on April 6, 2026, a portion of the Moon’s far side is seen along the terminator—the boundary between lunar day and night—where low-angle sunlight casts long shadows across the surface. A section of Orientale Basin is visible along the upper right portion of the lunar disk, its structure subtly revealed under […]

articles.title: Blue Origin hot fires its first previously flown booster, prepares for weekend launch
articles.summary: The engine test, called a static fire, saw the company ignite all seven of its BE-4 engines for less than half a minute. Local maritime and aviation hazards suggest a launch could happen as soon as Sunday, April 19.

articles.title: Artemis 2 is a benchmark in our space exploration program — and it depends on steady NASA funding
articles.summary:
Now that the Artemis 2 mission has been successfully completed, it’s worth taking a look at where NASA stands on the role of humans in exploring space and what its path forward should be. Doing this is especially important today, with some people questioning on line whether we can afford the cost of sending humans […]
The post Artemis 2 is a benchmark in our space exploration program — and it depends on steady NASA funding appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Three ESA-built satellites on show in France
articles.summary: Three Earth observation satellites, developed by the European Space Agency (ESA) with European partners, and due to launch later this year, have completed their functional and environmental tests and are ready to travel to the European spaceport in French Guiana. But first, journalists were invited to have one last look.
